border-orange-500","children":"Good lighting is the most underrated element of a productive workspace. Here are the desk lamps worth your money."}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"why-lighting-matters","children":"Why Lighting Matters"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Bad lighting doesn't just strain your eyes — it affects your mood, your energy levels, and the quality of your video calls."}}],["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The ideal desk lamp offers adjustable color temperature (so you can shift from energizing cool white during the day to relaxing warm white in the evening), adequate brightness without glare, and a form factor that doesn't eat up your desk."}}],["$","h2","3",{"id":"what-to-look-for","children":"What to Look For"}],["$","p","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Color temperature range</strong>: Look for 2700K–6500K. The wider the range, the more flexibility you have."}}],["$","p","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Brightness (lumens)</strong>: A desk lamp for task lighting should produce at least 400 lumens. More is useful if you work with paper or detailed physical materials."}}],"$L9","$La","$Lb","$Lc","$Ld","$Le","$Lf","$L10","$L11","$L12","$L13","$L14","$L15","$L16","$L17","$L18"]}],"$L19","$L1a","$L1b"]}],"$L1c"]}]]}]

a:["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>USB charging port</strong>: A small convenience that quickly becomes essential."}}]
b:["$","h2","8",{"id":"top-picks-for-2025","children":"Top Picks for 2025"}]
c:["$","h3","9",{"id":"best-overall-benq-screenbar-plus","children":"Best Overall: BenQ ScreenBar Plus"}]
d:["$","p","10",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Designed to sit on top of your monitor rather than taking up desk space. Asymmetric optical design lights your desk without illuminating the screen (which causes reflections). Has a controller dial for easy adjustment. Slightly expensive but genuinely unique."}}]
e:["$","h3","11",{"id":"best-traditional-lamp-elgato-key-light","children":"Best Traditional Lamp: Elgato Key Light"}]
f:["$","p","12",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Originally designed as a streaming light, the Key Light works brilliantly as a desk lamp. Very high CRI, app-controlled color temperature, and outputs serious brightness. A bit large, but excellent for video calls."}}]
10:["$","h3","13",{"id":"best-budget-taotronics-tt-dl13","children":"Best Budget: TaoTronics TT-DL13"}]
11:["$","p","14",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Adjustable arm, five color temperatures, seven brightness levels, and a USB charging port for under $35. Surprisingly capable for the price."}}]
12:["$","h3","15",{"id":"best-minimalist-anglepoise-type-75","children":"Best Minimalist: Anglepoise Type 75"}]
13:["$","p","16",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The classic British desk lamp. Not the most technically advanced option, but the build quality and adjustability are exceptional, and it looks extraordinary on any desk. A buy-once, use-forever object."}}]
14:["$","h3","17",{"id":"best-for-eye-strain-wilit-architect-desk-lamp","children":"Best for Eye Strain: WILIT Architect Desk Lamp"}]
15:["$","p","18",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A full-spectrum lamp with a CRI of 95+ and no visible flicker. Specifically designed for extended reading and detailed work. Excellent if you spend long hours reading physical documents."}}]
16:["$","h2","19",{"id":"placement-tips","children":"Placement Tips"}]
17:["$","p","20",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Position your lamp to the side of your monitor (left if you're right-handed, right if you're left-handed) to minimize glare and shadows. Angle it down onto the desk surface, not at the monitor."}}]
18:["$","p","21",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For video calls, position a lamp facing you (not behind you) to ensure your face is well-lit. The BenQ ScreenBar or Elgato Key Light are both excellent for this purpose."}}]
